message.content:
=== THE LAW (violating it burns the artifact regardless of score) ===
A source may enter the corpus ONLY bound to real bytes through provenance.py: manifestation_id +
content_hash, and for task 72's JOURNAL-ONLY policy it must be journal-attributable (a working paper is a
DISCOVERY LEAD, not journal evidence — peer review changes numbers; Acemoglu's robot effect was 0.37pp in
the NBER WP and 0.2pp in the JPE). Every fetch goes through acquisition.Acquirer, which emits observations,
never statuses. A 429 is THROTTLED -> BACKEND_FAILED, NEVER "no evidence exists".

=== THE TWO GUARDRAILS — WE HAVE HURT OURSELVES ON BOTH BEFORE ===
1. MAX THE SEARCH, NOT THE COUNT. Sol: the objective is NOT "100 papers" or "202 numbers" — it is ENOUGH
   INDEPENDENT, INTERPRETABLE EVIDENCE to close the question's cells and create high-value CONTRASTS.
   bodhi scores 0.5441 with ONE venue mention. So: cast the widest possible net, but ADMIT ONLY genuinely
   on-topic journal articles. A keyword net once dragged in ResNet, SMOTE, skin-cancer classification and
   the European Heart Journal ('work' is a substring of 'network'). Semantic on-topic gate, WORD BOUNDARIES.
2. GENERALITY. Run the CONTRACT-DRIVEN pipeline (research_contract -> source_router), NOT a hardcoded
   task-72 regex. We are running it ON task 72; we are not baking task 72 into the code.

=== DO NOT COLLIDE ===
Two other wheels are live (composer wiring, generality). Write ONLY to outputs/acquisition_campaign/.
Do NOT touch cellcog_composer.py, outputs/journal_corpus_content.json, or outputs/evidence_cards_bound.json.
acquisition.py's ledger is append-only union — safe to share.


=== WHERE WE ARE ===
10 admissible journal works (the honest post-quarantine corpus). cellcog #1 has ~98 sources; bodhi #2 ~33.
This is a FLOOR, not SOTA. The seed works, with DOIs to expand from:
  Acemoglu 2019 JEP | Goos 2014 AER | Damioli 2021 Eurasian Bus Rev | Schwabe 2020 PLOS ONE |
  Tolan 2021 J.AI Research | Cao 2021 Technovation | Braganza 2021 J.Bus.Research | Raj 2019 J.Org.Design |
  Ayling 2022 AI&Ethics | Chalmers 2021 Entrep.Theory&Practice

EMPTY COVERAGE CELLS (the outline promises sections we cannot support): healthcare, manufacturing, retail,
finance, education, agriculture, transport. And our corpus ENDS IN 2023 — the entire generative-AI turn
(2023-2025) is missing, and the judge rewards current evidence.

TOOLS ON DISK (read them, use them, do not rebuild): source_router.py (+ config/source_routes.yaml),
recency.py, gap_search.py, insight_value.py, weighting.py, journal_corpus_build.py (has citation-graph
expansion that once took us 22->70 papers), deep_fetch.py, wp_fetch.py, acquisition.py, provenance.py,
research_contract.py.


MODALITY 2 — OA-ROUTE DISCOVERY FOR THE EMPTY CELLS. Use source_router.py + config/source_routes.yaml to
route AI-and-work queries for EACH empty industry cell (healthcare, manufacturing, retail, finance,
education, agriculture, transport) to the right OA sources (DOAJ, OpenAlex, CORE, Europe PMC, Crossref).
This is where we are thinnest and where two criteria regressed (Industry Scope, Various Industries). Query
each cell specifically — "AI automation employment healthcare", "industrial robots manufacturing jobs",
"algorithmic management retail warehouse workers", "AI finance banking employment", etc. Keep only journal
articles genuinely on-topic (semantic gate, word boundaries). Return doi/title/venue/year/why_relevant/
cell_served, deduped, counts by cell. Do NOT fetch full text yet. If a cell genuinely has little journal
literature, SAY SO — an honest thin cell is a real finding.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""SOURCE ROUTER — CAPABILITY-BASED, DATA-DRIVEN, OA-FIRST DISCOVERY (Sol plan 4, item 2).
3	
4	THE STRATEGY THIS CHANGES
5	-------------------------
6	The old strategy was "pick the canonical (usually paywalled) work, then chase a free copy of it." It
7	had a 19% hit rate because it started from the version we were LEAST likely to be able to read. This
8	router inverts it: OA-FIRST DISCOVERY, then VERSION PURSUIT. We enumerate what is openly readable
9	first (DOAJ / PMC / Europe PMC / OpenAlex / CORE / OpenAIRE), resolve identity and the full set of
10	locations (Crossref / OpenAlex / Unpaywall — never trusting their version label), and only then pursue
11	the specific citable expression the plan's policy requires.
12	
13	WHAT MAKES IT GENERAL (and not the tell we keep failing)
14	--------------------------------------------------------
15	It does NOT ask an LLM to remember that economists use NBER or clinicians use PubMed. That knowledge is
16	a hardcoded topic gate — the exact defect this project keeps shipping. Here the knowledge lives in
17	`config/source_routes.yaml` as ONE ROW PER ROUTE, keyed by the EVIDENCE CAPABILITY (role) the route
18	supplies. This file is a GENERIC engine over that table:
19	
20	    contract  --(role_requirements table)-->  REQUIRED evidence roles
21	    required roles  --SET COVER over routes[*].evidence_roles-->  the routes that FIRE
22	
23	There is no adapter name and no topic word in this module. Adding a domain that these adapters already
24	serve — a new jurisdiction's courts, a new preprint server, a new registry — is a NEW ROW in the YAML
25	and ZERO lines of Python. That is the standing order: a domain change is a DATA edit, never a code edit.
26	
27	On a CLINICAL question it fires PubMed/PMC/Europe PMC/ClinicalTrials.gov and NOT NBER. On a LEGAL
28	question it fires GovInfo/EUR-Lex/CourtListener/SSRN and NOT NBER. On a THIN-EVIDENCE question it fires
29	the OA-first backbone, finds nothing, and — only if every applicable route genuinely ANSWERED — lets
30	the honest answer be "the literature we can reach does not settle this."
31	
32	FIRING IS NOT CITABILITY
33	------------------------
34	Whether a route FIRES (does the plan need its capability?) is a different decision from whether what it
35	returns is CITABLE (does the plan's source_policy permit that expression?). Under a journal-only policy,
36	arXiv and NBER still FIRE — for discovery and version pursuit — but their preprint/working-paper
37	expressions are NOT citable; we use them to locate the underlying study and pursue its journal Version
38	of Record. This module reports both, per route, and never collapses them.
39	
40	EVERY LIVE ATTEMPT GOES THROUGH acquisition.Acquirer
41	----------------------------------------------------
42	So every attempt lands on the append-only event ledger and the distinct outcomes stay distinct:
43	
44	    FETCHED | NOT_FOUND | ACCESS_DENIED | THROTTLED | TRANSIENT_ERROR | LANDING_PAGE | ABSTRACT_ONLY |
45	    WRONG_WORK | CORRUPT_EXTRACTION
46	
47	and — critically — the discovery outcome is DERIVED by a reducer over the ledger, never asserted by a
48	fetcher. Only NOT_FOUND, across every applicable route that genuinely answered, supports "we did not
49	locate an accessible copy." A 429 (THROTTLED) never does, and neither does a 403 (ACCESS_DENIED): those
50	are facts about OUR request and OUR entitlement, not about the world.
51	
52	Run it:
53	    python3 scripts/source_router.py                 # dry-runs (task 72, clinical, legal, thin) + outcome demo
54	    python3 scripts/source_router.py --live          # + a handful of real probes through Acquirer
55	    python3 scripts/source_router.py --routes         # print the route table as loaded
56	"""
57	from __future__ import annotations
58

74	from event_ledger import (  # noqa: E402
75	    EventKind, Ledger, observe_text,
76	    derive_content_profile, derive_semantic_binding,
77	    C_FULLTEXT, C_ABSTRACT, C_CITATION, C_NOT_DOC, C_UNREADABLE, DIFFERENT_WORK,

84	# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
85	# THE DISCOVERY-OUTCOME VOCABULARY — nine facts about a RETRIEVAL, none of them a fact about the world.
86	# They are DERIVED from the ledger below; a route may never set one.
87	# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
88	FETCHED            = 'FETCHED'             # a complete, readable document, and it IS the work we asked for
89	NOT_FOUND          = 'NOT_FOUND'           # the backend answered, and holds no accessible copy (404 / citation-only)
90	ACCESS_DENIED      = 'ACCESS_DENIED'       # 401/402/403/451 — a fact about ENTITLEMENT
91	THROTTLED          = 'THROTTLED'           # 429/503 — a fact about OUR REQUEST RATE. NEVER an absence.
92	TRANSIENT_ERROR    = 'TRANSIENT_ERROR'     # timeout / DNS / reset / 5xx / a hang — we never got an answer
93	LANDING_PAGE       = 'LANDING_PAGE'        # bytes that are a web page ABOUT the document, not the document
94	ABSTRACT_ONLY      = 'ABSTRACT_ONLY'       # a fragment of the article, not the article
95	WRONG_WORK         = 'WRONG_WORK'          # a complete document — of somebody else's paper
96	CORRUPT_EXTRACTION = 'CORRUPT_EXTRACTION'  # bytes we could not decode (a PDF of pure (cid:NN) glyphs)
97	NO_ATTEMPT         = 'NO_ATTEMPT'          # this route was never tried for this unit (not an outcome, a gap)
98	
99	#: The outcomes that mean "we HELD something" — presence, never absence.
100	_HELD = {FETCHED, ABSTRACT_ONLY, LANDING_PAGE, WRONG_WORK, CORRUPT_EXTRACTION}
101	#: The outcomes that are a fact about US, not the literature — they forbid an absence statement.
102	_OUR_FAULT = {THROTTLED, ACCESS_DENIED, TRANSIENT_ERROR}
103	
104	#: content class -> discovery outcome, once the transport RESPONDED and a manifestation was recorded.
105	_CLASS_TO_OUTCOME = {
106	    C_FULLTEXT: FETCHED,
107	    C_ABSTRACT: ABSTRACT_ONLY,
108	    C_CITATION: NOT_FOUND,       # a citation stub is not an accessible copy
109	    C_NOT_DOC: LANDING_PAGE,
110	    C_UNREADABLE: CORRUPT_EXTRACTION,
111	}
112

115	# THE ROUTE TABLE — loaded from data, never written in code.
116	# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
117	
118	@dataclass(frozen=True)
119	class Route:
120	    adapter_id: str
121	    evidence_roles: tuple[str, ...]
122	    document_types: tuple[str, ...]
123	    jurisdiction_coverage: tuple[str, ...]
124	    query_dialects: tuple[str, ...]
125	    identifier_types: tuple[str, ...]
126	    version_resolvers: tuple[str, ...]
127	    rate_policy: dict
128	    coverage_note: str
129	    endpoints: dict
130	    discovery_via: tuple[str, ...] = ()
131	    scope_ref: dict | None = None
132	
133	    @property
134	    def is_evidence_route(self) -> bool:
135	        """Does this route hand back a CITABLE document (vs. pure index/identity/location infrastructure)?"""
136	        return 'fetch.oa_fulltext' in self.evidence_roles or \
137	            any(r.startswith('evidence.') for r in self.evidence_roles)
138	
139	
140	@dataclass
141	class RouteTable:
142	    roles: dict[str, str]
143	    role_requirements: list[dict]
144	    jurisdiction_lexicon: dict[str, str]
145	    routes: list[Route]
146	    mailto: str
147	    registry_version: str = ''
148	
149	    def by_id(self, adapter_id: str) -> Route | None:
150	        return next((r for r in self.routes if r.adapter_id == adapter_id), None)
151	
152	    def validate(self) -> list[str]:
153	        """Every role a route supplies OR a requirement demands must be DEFINED. A dangling role would
154	        silently route nothing (or require the impossible), and print as an evidence gap. That is the
155	        failure mode this whole project exists to prevent, so the table refuses to load with one."""
156	        problems: list[str] = []
157	        defined = set(self.roles)
158	        for r in self.routes:
159	            for role in r.evidence_roles:
160	                if role not in defined:
161	                    problems.append(f'route {r.adapter_id!r} supplies undefined role {role!r}')
162	        for rr in self.role_requirements:
163	            if rr.get('role') not in defined:
164	                problems.append(f'role_requirement references undefined role {rr.get("role")!r}')
165	        return problems
166	
167	
168	def load_table(path: Path | str = ROUTES_YAML) -> RouteTable:
169	    import os
170	    raw = yaml.safe_load(Path(path).read_text(encoding='utf-8'))
171	    routes = []
172	    for row in raw.get('routes', []):
173	        routes.append(Route(
174	            adapter_id=row['adapter_id'],
175	            evidence_roles=tuple(row.get('evidence_roles', [])),
176	            document_types=tuple(row.get('document_types', [])),
177	            jurisdiction_coverage=tuple(row.get('jurisdiction_coverage', ['ANY'])),
178	            query_dialects=tuple(row.get('query_dialects', [])),
179	            identifier_types=tuple(row.get('identifier_types', [])),
180	            version_resolvers=tuple(row.get('version_resolvers', [])),
181	            rate_policy=dict(row.get('rate_policy', {})),
182	            coverage_note=(row.get('coverage_note', '') or '').strip(),
183	            endpoints=dict(row.get('endpoints', {}) or {}),
184	            discovery_via=tuple(row.get('discovery_via', []) or []),
185	            scope_ref=row.get('scope_ref'),
186	        ))
187	    mailto_env = raw.get('mailto_env', 'POLARIS_MAILTO')
188	    table = RouteTable(
189	        roles=dict(raw.get('roles', {})),
190	        role_requirements=list(raw.get('role_requirements', [])),
191	        jurisdiction_lexicon={str(k).lower(): v for k, v in (raw.get('jurisdiction_lexicon', {}) or {}).items()},
192	        routes=routes,
193	        mailto=os.environ.get(mailto_env, 'aldrin.or@c-polarbiotech.com'),
194	        registry_version=str(raw.get('registry_version', '')),
195	    )
196	    problems = table.validate()
197	    if problems:
198	        raise ValueError('source_routes.yaml is internally inconsistent:\n  ' + '\n  '.join(problems))
199	    return table

202	# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
203	# CONTRACT -> REQUIRED ROLES — a generic match of DATA tokens against the plan's own vocabulary.
204	# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
205	
206	def _get(contract: Any, name: str, default=None):
207	    """Read a field from a research_contract.Contract OR a plain dict. The router accepts either, so it
208	    can be exercised without importing the compiler."""
209	    if isinstance(contract, dict):
210	        return contract.get(name, default)
211	    return getattr(contract, name, default)
212	
213	
214	def _term_forms(terms: Iterable[Any]) -> list[str]:
215	    out: list[str] = []
216	    for t in terms or []:
217	        if isinstance(t, dict):
218	            out.append(str(t.get('label', '')))
219	            out.extend(str(a) for a in t.get('aliases', []) or [])
220	        else:
221	            out.append(str(getattr(t, 'label', '') or ''))
222	            out.extend(str(a) for a in getattr(t, 'aliases', []) or [])
223	    return out
224	
225	
226	def routing_text(contract: Any) -> str:
227	    """THE PLAN'S OWN VOCABULARY, assembled into one lowercased blob for the trigger matcher.
228	
229	    It is deliberately WIDE: the question and review subject (present even in a degraded, no-LLM
230	    contract), plus every structured field a full contract carries — method designs, unit levels,
231	    concepts and their surface aliases, framing devices, the subject axis, outcome dimensions,
232	    geographies and the genre. The richer the contract, the sharper the routing; but the question alone
233	    is enough to route, so an offline contract still works.
234	    """
235	    bits: list[str] = [
236	        str(_get(contract, 'question', '') or ''),
237	        str(_get(contract, 'review_subject', '') or ''),
238	        str(_get(contract, 'title', '') or ''),
239	        str(_get(contract, 'genre', '') or ''),
240	    ]
241	    bits += [str(x) for x in (_get(contract, 'method_designs', []) or [])]
242	    bits += [str(x) for x in (_get(contract, 'unit_levels', []) or [])]
243	    bits += [str(x) for x in (_get(contract, 'geographies', []) or [])]
244	    bits += [str(x) for x in (_get(contract, 'time_horizons', []) or [])]
245	    bits += [str(x) for x in (_get(contract, 'evidence_tuple', []) or [])]
246	    bits += _term_forms(_get(contract, 'core_concepts', []))
247	    bits += _term_forms(_get(contract, 'framing_devices', []))
248	    bits += _term_forms(_get(contract, 'outcome_dimensions', []))
249	    ax = _get(contract, 'subject_axis', None)
250	    if ax is not None:
251	        bits.append(str(_get(ax, 'name', '') or ''))
252	        bits += _term_forms(_get(ax, 'values', []))
253	    # a journal-only / peer-reviewed policy is itself a signal that peer-reviewed indices are wanted.
254	    sp = _get(contract, 'source_policy', None)
255	    if sp is not None:
256	        if _get(sp, 'peer_reviewed_only', False):
257	            bits.append('peer-reviewed journal articles')
258	        bits += [str(x) for x in (_get(sp, 'question_evidence', []) or [])]
259	    return re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', ' '.join(bits)).lower()
260	
261	
262	def _contains(text: str, token: str) -> bool:
263	    """Whole-word / whole-phrase containment. `wage` matches "wage" but not "wager"; "labor market"
264	    matches the phrase. The boundary is non-alphanumeric on both sides, so dotted forms like "u.s."
265	    match too. This is the ONLY pattern logic in the module, and it is topic-agnostic."""
266	    tok = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', token.strip().lower())
267	    if not tok:
268	        return False
269	    return re.search(rf'(?<![a-z0-9]){re.escape(tok)}(?![a-z0-9])', text) is not None
270	
271	
272	def required_roles(table: RouteTable, contract: Any) -> dict[str, str]:
273	    """The evidence roles this plan REQUIRES -> {role: basis}. `default: true` rules are the OA-first
274	    backbone (always required). The rest fire when the plan's routing text contains a trigger token."""
275	    text = routing_text(contract)
276	    out: dict[str, str] = {}
277	    for rule in table.role_requirements:
278	        role = rule.get('role')
279	        if rule.get('default'):
280	            out[role] = 'OA-first backbone (every scholarly plan needs it)'
281	            continue
282	        for tok in rule.get('any_of', []) or []:
283	            if _contains(text, tok):
284	                out[role] = f'plan vocabulary contains {tok!r}'
285	                break
286	    return out
287	
288	
289	def detect_jurisdictions(table: RouteTable, contract: Any) -> dict[str, str]:
290	    """{ISO code: the phrase that implied it}. Empty => the plan named no forum, and jurisdiction-scoped
291	    routes fire broadly (we do not know where to look, so we look everywhere applicable)."""
292	    text = routing_text(contract)
293	    found: dict[str, str] = {}
294	    for phrase, code in table.jurisdiction_lexicon.items():
295	        if _contains(text, phrase):
296	            found.setdefault(code, phrase)
297	    return found
298	
299	
300	def admissibility_policy(contract: Any) -> provenance.SourcePolicy:
301	    """WHICH EXPRESSION KINDS this answer may CITE — the plan's source_policy, made mechanical against
302	    provenance's version taxonomy. This is the axis that turns arXiv/NBER into discovery-only under a
303	    journal-only instruction, without stopping them from firing."""
304	    sp = _get(contract, 'source_policy', None)
305	    if sp is None or not _get(sp, 'peer_reviewed_only', False):
306	        return provenance.ANY_VERSION
307	    excluded = {str(x).lower() for x in (_get(sp, 'excluded_types', []) or [])}
308	    kinds = ['journal_version']
309	    if not any('proceeding' in e for e in excluded):
310	        kinds.append('proceedings_version')
311	    name = 'journal_articles_only' if kinds == ['journal_version'] else 'peer_reviewed'
312	    return provenance.SourcePolicy(name, tuple(kinds))
313	
314	
315	# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
316	# THE SET COVER — fire EVERY route that covers a required role, subject to the jurisdiction gate.
317	# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
318	
319	@dataclass
320	class FiredRoute:
321	    route: Route
322	    covered_roles: tuple[str, ...]     # the required roles THIS route supplies
323	    kind: str                          # 'evidence' | 'infrastructure'
324	    admissibility: str                 # 'citable' | 'discovery_only' | 'infrastructure'
325	    admissibility_reason: str = ''
326	
327	    @property
328	    def adapter_id(self) -> str:
329	        return self.route.adapter_id
330	
331	
332	@dataclass
333	class RoutingPlan:
334	    required: dict[str, str]
335	    jurisdictions: dict[str, str]
336	    policy: provenance.SourcePolicy
337	    fired: list[FiredRoute]
338	    jurisdiction_filtered: list[tuple[Route, str]]   # (route, reason) — supplied a role but wrong forum
339	    uncovered_roles: dict[str, str]                  # required role -> basis, with NO route to serve it
340	    per_role: dict[str, list[str]]                   # required role -> [adapter_ids covering it]
341	
342	    def fired_ids(self) -> list[str]:
343	        return [f.adapter_id for f in self.fired]
344	
345	    def fires(self, adapter_id: str) -> bool:
346	        return any(f.adapter_id == adapter_id for f in self.fired)
347	
348	
349	def _jurisdiction_ok(route: Route, jurisdictions: dict[str, str]) -> tuple[bool, str]:
350	    cov = set(route.jurisdiction_coverage)
351	    if 'ANY' in cov:
352	        return True, ''
353	    if not jurisdictions:
354	        return True, ''                      # no forum named -> fire broadly
355	    hit = cov & set(jurisdictions)
356	    if hit:
357	        return True, f'jurisdiction match {sorted(hit)}'
358	    return False, (f'covers {sorted(cov)} but the plan is scoped to {sorted(jurisdictions)} '
359	                   f'— wrong forum, not fired')
360	
361	
362	def route(table: RouteTable, contract: Any) -> RoutingPlan:
363	    """The whole routing decision. Pure over (table, contract): no network, deterministic, testable."""
364	    required = required_roles(table, contract)
365	    jurisdictions = detect_jurisdictions(table, contract)
366	    policy = admissibility_policy(contract)
367	    permitted = set(policy.permitted_expression_kinds)
368	
369	    fired: list[FiredRoute] = []
370	    filtered: list[tuple[Route, str]] = []
371	    per_role: dict[str, list[str]] = {r: [] for r in required}
372	
373	    for r in table.routes:
374	        covered = tuple(role for role in r.evidence_roles if role in required)
375	        if not covered:
376	            continue                          # this route serves nothing the plan needs
377	        ok, why = _jurisdiction_ok(r, jurisdictions)
378	        if not ok:
379	            filtered.append((r, why))
380	            continue
381	
382	        if r.is_evidence_route:
383	            citable = [dt for dt in r.document_types if dt in permitted]
384	            if citable:
385	                adm, reason = 'citable', f'yields {citable} — permitted by {policy.name}'
386	            else:
387	                adm, reason = 'discovery_only', (
388	                    f'yields {list(r.document_types)}; {policy.name} permits only '
389	                    f'{sorted(permitted)} — fires for DISCOVERY / version pursuit, output not citable')
390	            kind = 'evidence'
391	        else:
392	            adm, reason, kind = 'infrastructure', 'index / identity / location resolver', 'infrastructure'
393	
394	        fired.append(FiredRoute(r, covered, kind, adm, reason))
395	        for role in covered:
396	            per_role[role].append(r.adapter_id)
397	
398	    uncovered = {role: basis for role, basis in required.items() if not per_role.get(role)}
399	    return RoutingPlan(required, jurisdictions, policy, fired, filtered, uncovered, per_role)
400	
401	
402	# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
403	# THE DISCOVERY-OUTCOME REDUCER — over the ledger. A route never says how it did; the ledger does.
404	# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
405	
406	_REQUEST_KINDS = (EventKind.BACKEND_ATTEMPTED, EventKind.RESPONSE_RECEIVED,
407	                  EventKind.THROTTLED, EventKind.BLOCKED)
408	#: worst-first: any throttle/denial/error on an applicable request forbids reading absence off the rest.
409	_TRANSPORT_PRECEDENCE = (THROTTLED, ACCESS_DENIED, TRANSIENT_ERROR, 'RESPONDED', NOT_FOUND)
410	
411	
412	def _transport_outcome(events: list, adapter: str) -> str:
413	    """One adapter's transport-level result, read from the FINAL terminal event of each of its requests
414	    (so a 429-then-200 reads RESPONDED, not THROTTLED — the backoff worked). Distinguishes THROTTLED
415	    (429/503) from TRANSIENT_ERROR (timeout/5xx/hang), which the coarser event_ledger reducer folds
416	    together as BACKEND_FAILED but the discovery vocabulary must keep apart."""
417	    reqs: dict[str, list] = {}
418	    for e in events:
419	        if e.payload.get('adapter') != adapter or e.kind not in _REQUEST_KINDS:
420	            continue
421	        if 'derived_by' in e.payload:
422	            continue
423	        reqs.setdefault(str(e.payload.get('request_id') or ''), []).append(e)
424	    if not reqs:
425	        return NO_ATTEMPT
426	
427	    per: list[str] = []
428	    for _rid, evs in reqs.items():
429	        terminal = [e for e in evs if e.kind in (EventKind.RESPONSE_RECEIVED,
430	                                                  EventKind.THROTTLED, EventKind.BLOCKED)]
431	        if not terminal:
432	            per.append(TRANSIENT_ERROR)       # attempted, never came back — a HANG, not a gap
433	            continue
434	        e = terminal[-1]
435	        code = e.payload.get('http_status')
436	        if e.kind == EventKind.THROTTLED or code in (429, 503):
437	            per.append(THROTTLED)
438	        elif e.kind == EventKind.BLOCKED or code in (401, 402, 403, 451):
439	            per.append(ACCESS_DENIED)
440	        elif code == 404:
441	            per.append(NOT_FOUND)
442	        elif e.payload.get('transport_error'):
443	            per.append(TRANSIENT_ERROR)
444	        else:
445	            per.append('RESPONDED')
446	    for o in _TRANSPORT_PRECEDENCE:
447	        if o in per:
448	            return o
449	    return TRANSIENT_ERROR
450	
451	
452	def _content_outcome(events: list) -> tuple[str, str]:
453	    """When the transport RESPONDED, WHAT DID WE ACTUALLY GET? Derived from the SAME reducers the rest
454	    of the pipeline uses, so this module cannot disagree with the corpus about what a document is."""
455	    mans = [e for e in events
456	            if e.kind == EventKind.MANIFESTATION_FETCHED and 'derived_by' not in e.payload]
457	    if not mans:
458	        return NOT_FOUND, ('the backend answered but no document was fetched — an index/identity probe, '
459	                           'or a search that located no accessible copy')
460	    binding, binfo = derive_semantic_binding(events)
461	    if binding == DIFFERENT_WORK:
462	        return WRONG_WORK, binfo.get('reason', 'the fetched document is a different work')
463	    cls, info = derive_content_profile(events)
464	    return _CLASS_TO_OUTCOME.get(cls, NOT_FOUND), info.get('reason', '')
465	
466	
467	def classify_discovery_outcome(ledger: Ledger, unit: str, adapter: str) -> tuple[str, str]:
468	    """(outcome, basis) for one route's attempt to ACQUIRE A DOCUMENT for `unit`. Reduces over the
469	    ledger; asserts nothing. This is the function the pipeline calls to learn a route's honest result."""
470	    events = ledger.events(unit)
471	    t = _transport_outcome(events, adapter)
472	    if t == NO_ATTEMPT:
473	        return NO_ATTEMPT, 'this route was never attempted for this unit'
474	    if t != 'RESPONDED':
475	        basis = {
476	            THROTTLED: 'HTTP 429/503 — a fact about OUR REQUEST RATE, never about the literature',
477	            ACCESS_DENIED: 'HTTP 401/402/403/451 — a fact about ENTITLEMENT, never about the literature',
478	            NOT_FOUND: 'HTTP 404 — this backend does not index it (a fact about THEIR INDEX)',
479	            TRANSIENT_ERROR: 'timeout / DNS / reset / 5xx / hang — we never got an answer',
480	        }[t]
481	        return t, basis
482	    return _content_outcome(events)
483	
484	
485	def licenses_absence(outcomes: Iterable[str]) -> tuple[bool, str]:
486	    """May we write "we did not locate an accessible copy" given these per-route outcomes?
487	
488	    ONLY if every applicable route genuinely ANSWERED and none held a copy — i.e. the set is exactly
489	    {NOT_FOUND}. One THROTTLED, one ACCESS_DENIED, one TRANSIENT_ERROR, and the honest answer is
490	    SEARCH_FAILED: we do not know. This is the seven-way distinction of event_ledger, at the discovery
491	    layer: a 429 is not evidence of absence, and it never becomes one.
492	    """
493	    s = set(outcomes)
494	    if not s:
495	        return False, 'no applicable route was attempted — we never looked'
496	    if NO_ATTEMPT in s:
497	        return False, ('an applicable route was never attempted — the search is INCOMPLETE '
498	                       '(event_ledger: UNSEARCHED), which is not an absence')
499	    blockers = s & _OUR_FAULT
500	    if blockers:
501	        return False, (f'{sorted(blockers)} occurred — a fact about OUR request/entitlement, not the '
502	                       f'literature. Absence is NOT licensed; the honest status is SEARCH_FAILED')
503	    if FETCHED in s:
504	        return False, 'a copy was FETCHED — the claim here is PRESENCE, not absence'
505	    held = s & _HELD
506	    if held:
507	        return False, (f'we held a non-document ({sorted(held)}) — an abstract/landing page/wrong work '
508	                       f'is not a clean absence; pursue those before concluding')
509	    if s == {NOT_FOUND}:
510	        return True, ('every applicable route genuinely answered and none held an accessible copy — '
511	                      '"we did not locate an accessible copy" is licensed (event_ledger: SEARCHED_NONE)')
512	    return False, f'ambiguous outcome set {sorted(s)} — not a clean absence'
513

516	# THE LIVE DRIVER — builds a query URL from the route and fires it THROUGH acquisition.Acquirer.
517	# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
518	
519	def build_probe_url(route_: Route, table: RouteTable, *, kind: str = 'search',
520	                    query: str = '', doi: str = '', limit: int = 3, **extra) -> str | None:
521	    """Fill a route's endpoint template. Returns None if the route has no such endpoint (many
522	    separate-work routes are discovery_via another route and have no direct search API)."""
523	    tmpl = (route_.endpoints or {}).get(kind)
524	    if not tmpl:
525	        return None
526	    params = {
527	        'query': urllib.parse.quote(query or ''),
528	        'doi': urllib.parse.quote(doi or '', safe=''),
529	        'mailto': urllib.parse.quote(table.mailto, safe='@.'),
530	        'limit': str(limit),
531	        **{k: urllib.parse.quote(str(v), safe='') for k, v in extra.items()},
532	    }
533	    try:
534	        return tmpl.format(**params)
535	    except KeyError:
536	        return None                          # template needs a param we do not have (e.g. an api key)
537	
538	
539	def _count_candidates(payload: Any) -> int | None:
540	    """Best-effort candidate count across the OA APIs' differing envelopes. Informational only."""
541	    if not isinstance(payload, dict):
542	        return None
543	    for path in (('meta', 'count'), ('hitCount',)):
544	        node = payload
545	        for k in path:
546	            node = node.get(k) if isinstance(node, dict) else None
547	        if isinstance(node, int):
548	            return node
549	    for k in ('results', 'records', 'studies', 'data'):
550	        v = payload.get(k)
551	        if isinstance(v, list):
552	            return len(v)
553	    msg = payload.get('message')
554	    if isinstance(msg, dict) and isinstance(msg.get('items'), list):
555	        return len(msg['items'])
556	    rl = payload.get('resultList')
557	    if isinstance(rl, dict) and isinstance(rl.get('result'), list):
558	        return len(rl['result'])
559	    return None
560	
561	
562	def live_index_probe(acq, table: RouteTable, route_: Route, unit: str, *,
563	                     query: str = '', doi: str = '', limit: int = 2) -> dict:
564	    """Fire ONE index/identity probe through Acquirer and report the TRANSPORT-level discovery outcome.
565	
566	    This proves the wiring: the attempt is on the ledger, and the outcome is DERIVED from it (THROTTLED
567	    stays THROTTLED, a 404 stays NOT_FOUND). It is NOT a document fetch, so it never yields FETCHED —
568	    the document-level outcomes are exercised by the deterministic reducer demo. Kept to a handful, at
569	    Acquirer's per-host spacing, per Sol's "prove routing with a dry run + a few live probes."
570	    """
571	    kind = 'search' if 'search' in route_.endpoints else ('identity' if 'identity' in route_.endpoints else None)
572	    if not kind:
573	        return {'adapter': route_.adapter_id, 'skipped': 'no direct search/identity endpoint (discovery_via another route)'}
574	    url = build_probe_url(route_, table, kind=kind, query=query, doi=doi, limit=limit)
575	    if not url:
576	        return {'adapter': route_.adapter_id, 'skipped': 'endpoint needs an API key / param we do not hold'}
577	    r, payload = acq.get_json(unit, route_.adapter_id, url, tries=2, timeout=20)
578	    t = _transport_outcome(acq.ledger.events(unit), route_.adapter_id)
579	    label = 'ANSWERED' if t == 'RESPONDED' else t
580	    return {'adapter': route_.adapter_id, 'kind': kind, 'transport': label,
581	            'http': r.http_status, 'candidates': _count_candidates(payload),
582	            'url': url[:88] + ('…' if len(url) > 88 else '')}
583

589	def render_plan(plan: RoutingPlan, table: RouteTable, *, title: str) -> str:
590	    L: list[str] = []
591	    W = 100
592	    L.append('═' * W)
593	    L.append(title)
594	    L.append('═' * W)
595	    j = ', '.join(f'{c} (from {p!r})' for c, p in plan.jurisdictions.items()) or 'none named — fire broadly'
596	    L.append(f'  citation policy : {plan.policy.name}  (may cite: {", ".join(plan.policy.permitted_expression_kinds)})')
597	    L.append(f'  jurisdictions   : {j}')
598	    L.append(f'  required roles  : {len(plan.required)}')
599	    for role, basis in plan.required.items():
600	        cover = plan.per_role.get(role) or []
601	        mark = '  ' if cover else '!!'
602	        L.append(f'    {mark} {role:<34} <- {basis}')
603	        L.append(f'         covered by: {", ".join(cover) if cover else "NOTHING (capability gap — reported, never a false evidence gap)"}')
604	    ev = [f for f in plan.fired if f.kind == 'evidence']
605	    inf = [f for f in plan.fired if f.kind == 'infrastructure']
606	    L.append('')
607	    L.append(f'  ROUTES THAT FIRE : {len(plan.fired)}   (evidence {len(ev)} · infrastructure {len(inf)})')
608	    L.append('  ── evidence routes ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────')
609	    for f in sorted(ev, key=lambda x: (x.admissibility, x.adapter_id)):
610	        tag = 'CITABLE       ' if f.admissibility == 'citable' else 'discovery-only'
611	        L.append(f'    [{tag}] {f.adapter_id:<20} {",".join(f.covered_roles)}')
612	        L.append(f'                     └ {f.admissibility_reason}')
613	    L.append('  ── infrastructure (index / identity / locations) ────────────────────────────────────')
614	    L.append('    ' + ', '.join(f.adapter_id for f in inf))
615	    if plan.jurisdiction_filtered:
616	        L.append('  ── filtered by jurisdiction (supplied a role, wrong forum) ──────────────────────────')
617	        for r, why in plan.jurisdiction_filtered:
618	            L.append(f'    {r.adapter_id:<20} {why}')
619	    return '\n'.join(L)

626	def route_question(question: str, *, question_id: int | None = None, use_llm: bool = False,
627	                   table: RouteTable | None = None) -> tuple[Any, RoutingPlan, RouteTable]:
628	    """Compile a contract (offline by default) and route it. Returns (contract, plan, table)."""
629	    from research_contract import compile_contract
630	    table = table or load_table()
631	    contract = compile_contract(question, question_id=question_id, use_llm=use_llm, verbose=False)
632	    return contract, route(table, contract), table

639	# The three questions the standing order requires a mechanism to state its behaviour on — plus a
640	# thin-evidence one, where "the literature does not settle this" is the CORRECT answer. NONE of them is
641	# wired into the router: each is compiled to a contract and routed by the SAME data table.
642	_Q_TASK72 = ('Please write a literature review on the restructuring impact of Artificial Intelligence '
643	             '(AI) on the labor market. Focus on how AI, as a key driver of the Fourth Industrial '
644	             'Revolution, is causing significant disruptions and affecting various industries. Ensure '
645	             'the review only cites high-quality, English-language journal articles.')
646	_Q_CLINICAL = ('What is the comparative efficacy and cardiovascular safety of SGLT2 inhibitors versus '
647	               'GLP-1 receptor agonists in adults with type 2 diabetes? Synthesise the evidence from '
648	               'randomized controlled trials and current clinical practice guidelines.')
649	_Q_LEGAL = ('How have United States federal and state courts allocated tort liability for personal '
650	            'injuries caused by autonomous vehicle systems? Analyse the leading case law and the '
651	            'relevant federal statutes and regulations.')
652	_Q_THIN = ('What is known about the long-term neurological effects of chronic exposure to sub-acoustic '
653	           'infrasound from onshore wind turbines in human populations?')

655	
656	def _words(n: int, seed: str = '') -> str:
657	    base = ('the estimated effect of automation on employment varies across occupations and industries '
658	            'with routine cognitive tasks most exposed while wages and productivity respond over the '
659	            'medium run subject to considerable uncertainty in the underlying identification strategy ')
660	    out = (seed + ' ') if seed else ''
661	    while len(out.split()) < n:
662	        out += base
663	    return ' '.join(out.split()[:n])

666	def _emit_fetch(L: Ledger, unit: str, adapter: str, text: str, *, requested_title: str = '',
667	                requested_authors: tuple[str, ...] = (), source_type: str = '') -> None:
668	    """A transport RESPONSE plus a recorded document — the shape acquisition.record_manifestation
669	    writes, minus the blob store. The content profile is computed by observe_text, exactly as in
670	    production, so the reducer sees a real profile and not a hand-set label."""
671	    rid = f'{adapter}#demo'
672	    L.emit(unit, EventKind.BACKEND_ATTEMPTED, 'router-demo', adapter=adapter, url='https://example/x',
673	           request_id=rid, attempt=1)
674	    L.emit(unit, EventKind.RESPONSE_RECEIVED, 'router-demo', adapter=adapter, url='https://example/x',
675	           request_id=rid, attempt=1, http_status=200, n_bytes=len(text.encode()))
676	    L.emit(unit, EventKind.MANIFESTATION_FETCHED, 'router-demo', adapter=adapter,
677	           locator='https://example/x', requested_title=requested_title,
678	           requested_authors=list(requested_authors), source_type=source_type)
679	    L.emit(unit, EventKind.CONTENT_PROFILE_DERIVED, 'observe_text', **observe_text(text))
680	
681	
682	def _emit_transport(L: Ledger, unit: str, adapter: str, outcome: str) -> None:
683	    """A transport FAILURE with no document — the four ways an attempt can end without an answer."""
684	    rid = f'{adapter}#demo'
685	    L.emit(unit, EventKind.BACKEND_ATTEMPTED, 'router-demo', adapter=adapter, url='https://example/x',
686	           request_id=rid, attempt=1)
687	    if outcome == THROTTLED:
688	        L.emit(unit, EventKind.THROTTLED, 'router-demo', adapter=adapter, url='https://example/x',
689	               request_id=rid, attempt=1, http_status=429)
690	    elif outcome == ACCESS_DENIED:
691	        L.emit(unit, EventKind.BLOCKED, 'router-demo', adapter=adapter, url='https://example/x',
692	               request_id=rid, attempt=1, http_status=403)
693	    elif outcome == NOT_FOUND:
694	        L.emit(unit, EventKind.RESPONSE_RECEIVED, 'router-demo', adapter=adapter, url='https://example/x',
695	               request_id=rid, attempt=1, http_status=404, n_bytes=0)
696	    elif outcome == TRANSIENT_ERROR:
697	        L.emit(unit, EventKind.RESPONSE_RECEIVED, 'router-demo', adapter=adapter, url='https://example/x',
698	               request_id=rid, attempt=1, transport_error='TimeoutError', reason_text='timed out')
699

701	def _demo_outcomes() -> int:
702	    """Build one synthetic ledger that exercises ALL NINE discovery outcomes, DERIVE each from the
703	    ledger, and prove the absence-licensing invariant: only NOT_FOUND everywhere licenses absence; one
704	    429 never does. Deterministic, offline, and it fails loud if any mapping drifts."""
705	    print('\n' + '═' * 100)
706	    print('DISCOVERY-OUTCOME REDUCER — nine distinct outcomes, each DERIVED from the ledger (no asserts)')
707	    print('═' * 100)
708	    L = Ledger()   # in-memory, no path: this derives, it does not persist
709	
710	    cases: list[tuple[str, str, str]] = []   # (unit, adapter, expected)
711	    _emit_fetch(L, 'w:fulltext', 'europe_pmc',
712	                _words(1600, 'Effects of Automation on Employment. By Daron Acemoglu and Pascual Restrepo.'),
713	                requested_title='Effects of Automation on Employment', requested_authors=('Acemoglu',),
714	                source_type='journal-article')
715	    cases.append(('w:fulltext', 'europe_pmc', FETCHED))
716	
717	    _emit_fetch(L, 'w:opinion', 'courtlistener',
718	                'Doe v. Acme Autonomous Systems. Held: the operator of an autonomous vehicle system '
719	                'bears liability for a foreseeable failure it did not disclose. The judgment of the '
720	                'district court is affirmed.',
721	                requested_title='Doe v Acme Autonomous Systems Liability', source_type='opinion')
722	    cases.append(('w:opinion', 'courtlistener', FETCHED))   # complete at 40 words — a judicial opinion
723	
724	    _emit_fetch(L, 'w:abstract', 'openalex', _words(300, 'Automation and Wages. By A. Author.'),
725	                requested_title='Automation and Wages', requested_authors=('Author',),
726	                source_type='journal-article')
727	    cases.append(('w:abstract', 'openalex', ABSTRACT_ONLY))
728	
729	    _emit_fetch(L, 'w:stub', 'crossref', 'Automation and Wages. Journal of Labor Economics 2021.',
730	                requested_title='Automation and Wages', requested_authors=('Author',),
731	                source_type='journal-article')
732	    cases.append(('w:stub', 'crossref', NOT_FOUND))         # a citation stub is not an accessible copy
733	
734	    _emit_fetch(L, 'w:landing', 'pmc',
735	                'This website uses cookies. Accept all cookies. Subscribe to read the full article. '
736	                'Sign in to continue. Privacy policy. Terms of use. All rights reserved.',
737	                requested_title='Automation and Wages', source_type='journal-article')
738	    cases.append(('w:landing', 'pmc', LANDING_PAGE))
739	
740	    _emit_fetch(L, 'w:corrupt', 'core', '(cid:3) (cid:7) (cid:12) (cid:19) ' * 80,
741	                requested_title='Automation and Wages', source_type='journal-article')
742	    cases.append(('w:corrupt', 'core', CORRUPT_EXTRACTION))
743	
744	    _emit_fetch(L, 'w:wrong', 'openalex',
745	                'Mathematics and the Rise of the Machines: Automated Theorem Proving. By Yang-Hui He. '
746	                'This paper develops a neural approach to formal proof search in algebraic geometry.',
747	                requested_title='rise machines', requested_authors=('Parry',),
748	                source_type='journal-article')
749	    cases.append(('w:wrong', 'openalex', WRONG_WORK))
750	
751	    _emit_transport(L, 'w:denied', 'arxiv', ACCESS_DENIED)
752	    cases.append(('w:denied', 'arxiv', ACCESS_DENIED))
753	    _emit_transport(L, 'w:throttled', 'crossref', THROTTLED)
754	    cases.append(('w:throttled', 'crossref', THROTTLED))
755	    _emit_transport(L, 'w:timeout', 'doaj', TRANSIENT_ERROR)
756	    cases.append(('w:timeout', 'doaj', TRANSIENT_ERROR))
757	    _emit_transport(L, 'w:missing', 'openalex', NOT_FOUND)
758	    cases.append(('w:missing', 'openalex', NOT_FOUND))
759	
760	    ok = True
761	    for unit, adapter, expected in cases:
762	        got, basis = classify_discovery_outcome(L, unit, adapter)
763	        flag = 'ok ' if got == expected else 'XX '
764	        ok = ok and got == expected
765	        print(f'  {flag}{got:<18} (expected {expected:<18}) {adapter:<13} {unit}')
766	        print(f'       └ {basis[:92]}')
767	    if not ok:
768	        print('\n  !! A DISCOVERY-OUTCOME MAPPING DRIFTED — see the XX rows above.')
769	        return 1
770	
771	    print('\n  ── absence-licensing invariant (the 429-is-never-a-gap rule, at the discovery layer) ──')
772	    scenarios = [
773	        ('every applicable route answered 404', [NOT_FOUND, NOT_FOUND, NOT_FOUND]),
774	        ('same, but ONE route was throttled',   [NOT_FOUND, NOT_FOUND, THROTTLED]),
775	        ('same, but ONE route was blocked',     [NOT_FOUND, ACCESS_DENIED]),
776	        ('one route returned an abstract',      [NOT_FOUND, ABSTRACT_ONLY]),
777	        ('a copy was fetched',                  [FETCHED, NOT_FOUND]),
778	        ('an applicable route was never tried', [NOT_FOUND, NO_ATTEMPT]),
779	    ]
780	    inv_ok = True
781	    for label, outs in scenarios:
782	        lic, why = licenses_absence(outs)
783	        expect = (label == 'every applicable route answered 404')
784	        flag = 'ok ' if lic == expect else 'XX '
785	        inv_ok = inv_ok and lic == expect
786	        print(f'  {flag}absence {"LICENSED " if lic else "REFUSED  "} :: {label}')
787	        print(f'       └ {why[:92]}')
788	    return 0 if (ok and inv_ok) else 1

791	def _live_probes(table: RouteTable) -> None:
792	    """A handful of REAL index/identity probes through acquisition.Acquirer — proof of wiring, not a
793	    crawl. Each lands on a throwaway ledger; the transport-level outcome is DERIVED from it. Failures
794	    (a locked-down egress, a rate limit) print as the honest outcome, never as a router error."""
795	    import tempfile
796	    from acquisition import Acquirer, BlobStore
797	    print('\n' + '═' * 100)
798	    print('LIVE PROBES THROUGH acquisition.Acquirer (index/identity only; a few, politely spaced)')
799	    print('═' * 100)
800	    tmp = Path(tempfile.mkdtemp(prefix='sr_probe_'))
801	    acq = Acquirer('source_router.live_probe', ledger=Ledger(tmp / 'ledger.jsonl'),
802	                   blobs=BlobStore(tmp / 'blobs'))
803	    probes = [
804	        ('openalex', 'search', dict(query='artificial intelligence labor market', limit=2)),
805	        ('crossref', 'identity', dict(doi='10.1257/aer.103.5.1553')),
806	        ('unpaywall', 'identity', dict(doi='10.1257/aer.103.5.1553')),
807	        ('doaj', 'search', dict(query='artificial intelligence employment', limit=2)),
808	        ('europe_pmc', 'search', dict(query='SGLT2 inhibitors cardiovascular', limit=2)),
809	        ('clinicaltrials_gov', 'search', dict(query='SGLT2 inhibitors type 2 diabetes', limit=2)),
810	        ('courtlistener', 'search', dict(query='autonomous vehicle liability', limit=2)),
811	    ]
812	    for adapter_id, _kind, params in probes:
813	        r = table.by_id(adapter_id)
814	        if r is None:
815	            continue
816	        try:
817	            res = live_index_probe(acq, table, r, f'probe:{adapter_id}', **params)
818	        except Exception as e:                 # a probe may fail; the router must not
819	            res = {'adapter': adapter_id, 'error': f'{type(e).__name__}: {str(e)[:60]}'}
820	        if 'skipped' in res:
821	            print(f'  --  {adapter_id:<20} skipped: {res["skipped"]}')
822	        elif 'error' in res:
823	            print(f'  ..  {adapter_id:<20} {res["error"]}')
824	        else:
825	            cand = res.get('candidates')
826	            cand_s = f'{cand} candidates' if cand is not None else 'candidates: n/a'
827	            print(f'  ->  {adapter_id:<20} {res["transport"]:<16} http={res.get("http")}  {cand_s}')
828	    print(f'\n  ledger: {tmp / "ledger.jsonl"}  (every attempt above is on it, distinct and un-collapsible)')
829
